generatedFrom: github.com/carbon-design-system/carbon-ai-chat @carbon/ai-chat v1.15.0 — facts verified against src TypeScript types on main (PublicConfig.ts, MessagingConfig.ts, Messages.ts, ChatInstance.ts, eventBusTypes.ts, component/ChatContainer.ts) and the examples/ apps.
summary: @carbon/ai-chat is IBM Carbon's front-end chat framework — an opinionated, extensible chat UI shipped in BOTH React (<ChatContainer>, <ChatCustomElement>) and web-component (<cds-aichat-container>, <cds-aichat-custom-element>) forms from one ESM-only package. It is BACKEND-AGNOSTIC: you own the LLM/server call inside a messaging.customSendMessage callback and push responses back through the ChatInstance. It is NOT hardwired to watsonx (the watsonx avatar/name is just an overridable default). It renders in shadow DOM and self-injects Carbon theme tokens, so there is no mandatory CSS import.

packages.@carbon/ai-chat: v1.15.0 — the published chat framework (React + web components). ESM-only ("type":"module").
packages.@carbon/ai-chat-components: v1.5.0 — lower-level Carbon-compliant chat building-block components for composing/extending your own widget.

peerDeps.@carbon/web-components: >=2.54.0 <3.0.0 (REQUIRED peer — the web-component build is the foundation; install it alongside)
peerDeps.react: >=17.0.0 <20.0.0 (peer — React 17/18/19; there is an examples/react/react-17 app)

setup.reactImport: import { ChatContainer, ChatCustomElement, type PublicConfig, type ChatInstance, CarbonTheme, MessageResponseTypes, BusEventType } from '@carbon/ai-chat';
setup.webComponentImport: import '@carbon/ai-chat/dist/es/web-components/cds-aichat-container/index.js'; // side-effect: registers <cds-aichat-container>. Types/enums still import from '@carbon/ai-chat'.
setup.css: No CSS import is required for the chat itself — it renders in shadow DOM and injects its own Carbon tokens. (Optional: import '@carbon/styles/css/styles.css' to give the HOST PAGE IBM Plex + reset; that is the separate @carbon/styles package, not @carbon/ai-chat.) The only conditional stylesheet from this package is '@carbon/ai-chat/css/chat-float-layout.css', and only if you hand-apply float classes to ChatCustomElement (ChatContainer wires it for you).
setup.esm: ESM-only package ("type":"module", no CommonJS main). Your bundler must handle ESM.
setup.node: Repo dev toolchain pins Node 24.15.0 (.nvmrc); the package declares no consumer engines floor.
setup.nextjs: Client-only: the chat touches window + custom elements. Mark the module 'use client' AND load it with next/dynamic(() => import('./Chat'), { ssr: false }). Pattern is from examples/react/next (Next.js App Router).
setup.configIdentity: Re-creating the config object on every render restarts the chat (deep-equal diff). Define config outside render or memoize messaging/handlers.

configFields.purpose: The backend wiring: customSendMessage (receive user turn), customLoadHistory, messageTimeoutSecs (default 150; 0=never), skipWelcome, showStopButtonImmediately. THE field you must set.
Force a theme: CarbonTheme.WHITE | G10 | G90 | G100. If unset, the chat inherits the host page's Carbon theme (falls back to WHITE / dark-by-background).
Carbon AI theme styling (the AI gradient/AI label treatment). Defaults to true.
{ isOn, disclaimerHTML } shown before first use.
Open the chat on load instead of showing only the launcher.
Render a transcript with no input.
(<=30 chars) Disambiguate multiple chat instances on one page.
Human-agent handoff: return your ServiceDesk implementation.
(experimental) File upload: is_on, accept, maxFiles, onFileUpload.
Error hook (OnErrorType: MESSAGE_COMMUNICATION | RENDER | INTEGRATION_ERROR | HYDRATION).

events: pre:receive / receive — a MessageResponse is about to be / was rendered (event.data: MessageResponse).
pre:send / send — a user MessageRequest is about to be / was sent.
view:pre:change / view:change — chat opening/closing (BusEventViewChange.cancelViewChange to veto).
chat:ready — the instance has finished initializing.
userDefinedResponse / chunk:userDefinedResponse — fill a user_defined slot (web-component / event path).
feedback — thumbs up/down on a response (FeedbackInteractionType).
stopStreaming — user pressed the stop-streaming button.
human_agent:pre:receive / human_agent:receive / human_agent:pre:send / human_agent:send / human_agent:pre:startChat / human_agent:pre:endChat / human_agent:endChat — service-desk lifecycle.

instanceApi.obtain: React: pass onBeforeRender={(chat) => setInstance(chat)} to <ChatContainer>. Web component: set .onBeforeRender on the element.
instanceApi.messaging.addMessage: (message: MessageResponse) => Promise<void> — push a complete (non-streamed) bot response.
instanceApi.messaging.addMessageChunk: (chunk: StreamChunk) => Promise<void> — stream a response; chunk is PartialItemChunk {partial_item} | CompleteItemChunk {complete_item} | FinalResponseChunk {final_response}, merged by streaming_metadata.id.
instanceApi.send: (message: MessageRequest | string, options?: { silent?: boolean }) => Promise<void> — programmatically send a turn.
instanceApi.on / off / once: ({ type: BusEventType, handler }) => EventHandlers — subscribe to the event bus.
instanceApi.getState: () => PublicChatState — immutable snapshot (activeResponseId, input, loading counters, humanAgent, customPanels).
instanceApi.writeableElements: Partial<WriteableElements> — HTMLElements for HEADER_BOTTOM, BEFORE_INPUT, AFTER_INPUT, WELCOME_NODE_BEFORE, FOOTER, etc. React: feed via renderWriteableElements prop.

antiPatterns: Treating it like a watsonx Assistant SDK. It is NOT — there is no built-in assistant API. You implement messaging.customSendMessage and call your own backend/LLM, then instance.messaging.addMessage(...). The watsonx name/avatar are overridable defaults only.
Importing a CSS file to 'fix' unstyled chat. The chat is in shadow DOM and self-injects Carbon tokens; @carbon/styles is for the host page, not the widget. Don't invent '@carbon/ai-chat/styles.css'.
Rendering it during SSR. It needs window + custom elements — in Next.js use 'use client' + next/dynamic({ssr:false}), or it throws on the server.
Re-creating the config/messaging object inline in render. The deep-equal diff restarts the chat. Define config/handlers outside render or memoize them.
Forgetting the @carbon/web-components peer dependency (>=2.54). The chat is built on it; missing it breaks the build.

Hand-rolling rich content as raw HTML strings. Use the typed GenericItem response types (card, carousel, button, grid, conversational_search) or a user_defined item with renderUserDefinedResponse for your own React — don't bypass the model.
Hardcoding colors to theme the widget. Force a theme with injectCarbonTheme and recolor the shell via --cds-aichat-* / --cds-* custom properties, not literal hex.
Building a custom 'talk to a human' flow from scratch. Use serviceDeskFactory + the ServiceDesk interface (startChat/sendMessageToAgent/endChat) and the human_agent:* events.

topics.summary: Install @carbon/ai-chat (+ the @carbon/web-components peer), import ChatContainer, give it a PublicConfig with messaging.customSendMessage. No CSS import needed (shadow DOM self-injects tokens).
topics.detail: ESM-only. React: <ChatContainer {...config} /> or the sized <ChatCustomElement className=... />. Web component: side-effect import the cds-aichat-container entry and use <cds-aichat-container .messaging=${...} .onBeforeRender=${...}>. The only optional stylesheet is @carbon/ai-chat/css/chat-float-layout.css for manual float layout.

topics.summary: You receive the user's turn in messaging.customSendMessage(request, options, instance) and reply by calling instance.messaging.addMessage(...) with a MessageResponse whose output.generic is an array of GenericItem. Backend-agnostic — call your own server/LLM here.
topics.detail: customSendMessage may be async; if it returns a Promise the widget queues later user messages until it resolves, and rejection shows an error. options.signal is an AbortSignal that fires on messageTimeoutSecs (default 150) or a stop press. Load prior turns with messaging.customLoadHistory(instance) => HistoryItem[].

topics.summary: Stream a response with instance.messaging.addMessageChunk(chunk). Send PartialItemChunk {partial_item} pieces (merged by streaming_metadata.id), then a FinalResponseChunk {final_response} to finalize. CompleteItemChunk {complete_item} finalizes a single item.
topics.detail: Each item carries streaming_metadata { id, cancellable?, stream_stopped? }. The final_response.id must match streaming_metadata.response_id. MessageState is STREAMING | COMPLETE | ERROR. Honor options.signal (AbortSignal) to stop streaming.

topics.summary: A bot response is a MessageResponse with output.generic: GenericItem[]. Each item has a response_type (text, option, button, card, carousel, grid, conversational_search, image/video/audio, iframe, date, user_defined, connect_to_agent, …). Use the typed items rather than raw HTML.
topics.detail: Use carbon_ai_chat with responseType='<type>' to get the exact item interface and a snippet. text supports markdown; option renders buttons/dropdown; conversational_search is the RAG citation type; user_defined is your own React (see the user-defined topic).

topics.summary: Render your own React inside a response: emit a GenericItem with response_type 'user_defined' (payload in its user_defined field) from the backend, and pass renderUserDefinedResponse to <ChatContainer> to return a ReactNode for it.
topics.detail: renderUserDefinedResponse(state, instance) => ReactNode, where state = { messageItem?, partialItems?, fullMessage?, state? (STREAMING|COMPLETE|ERROR) }. Web component: set .renderUserDefinedResponse returning an HTMLElement, or listen for BusEventType.USER_DEFINED_RESPONSE and fill the named slot.

topics.summary: Force a Carbon theme with injectCarbonTheme: CarbonTheme.WHITE|G10|G90|G100 (else it inherits the host page). aiEnabled (default true) controls the Carbon AI gradient styling. Recolor the shell via --cds-aichat-* and --cds-* CSS custom properties — never literal hex.
topics.detail: WHITE/G10 are light, G90/G100 dark. Carbon tokens (--cds-link-primary, --cds-button-primary, …) and chat-shell tokens (--cds-aichat-launcher-color-background, …) layer together.

topics.summary: Float vs full-area is a COMPONENT choice, not a flag. ChatContainer / <cds-aichat-container> = floating launcher + popover. ChatCustomElement / <cds-aichat-custom-element> = you size/place it via className (sidebar, fullscreen, nested). Tune sizing with layout.customProperties and layout.corners.
topics.detail: ChatCustomElement is 0x0 until opened; use its onViewChange/onViewPreChange props to set size and animate. layout.corners uses CornersType.ROUND|SQUARE (per-corner: startStart/startEnd/endStart/endEnd).

topics.summary: The chat is client-only (needs window + custom elements). In Next.js App Router, mark the chat module 'use client' and load it with next/dynamic({ ssr: false }) so it never renders on the server.
topics.detail: Pattern from examples/react/next. Also keep the config object stable (define outside render / memoize) so re-renders don't restart the chat.

topics.summary: Capture the ChatInstance via onBeforeRender (React prop / web-component property). It exposes messaging.addMessage(Chunk), send(), changeView(), on/off/once(event bus), getState(), customPanels, writeableElements, and serviceDesk actions.
topics.detail: Subscribe to events with instance.on({ type: BusEventType.RECEIVE, handler }). Open/close programmatically with changeView(ViewType.MAIN_WINDOW). See the events topic for the BusEventType list.

topics.summary: An event bus on the instance (on/off/once) with a BusEventType enum: pre:receive/receive, pre:send/send, view:pre:change/view:change, chat:ready, state:change, feedback, stopStreaming, history:*, restartConversation, and human_agent:* for service desk.
topics.detail: Handlers are (event, instance) => unknown (may be async; the bus awaits). view:pre:change can veto opening/closing via cancelViewChange. See examples/react/watch-state for state:change.

topics.summary: Hand off to a human via serviceDeskFactory: (params) => Promise<ServiceDesk>. Implement the ServiceDesk interface (startChat, sendMessageToAgent, endChat, areAnyAgentsOnline?) and drive the UI through the provided ServiceDeskCallback (agentJoined, sendMessageToUser, agentTyping, agentEndedChat).
topics.detail: Emit a connect_to_agent response to offer the handoff. Tune with serviceDesk: { availabilityTimeoutSeconds, agentJoinTimeoutSeconds, allowReconnect }. Lifecycle is observable via human_agent:* events. See examples/{react,web-components}/human-agent.

responseTypes.fields: text?: string (CommonMark + extensions; HTML allowed unless shouldSanitizeHTML)
responseTypes.use: Plain or markdown assistant text. The default response.

responseTypes.fields: title?, description?, options: SingleOption[] ({ label, value: { input: { text } } }), preference?: 'dropdown'|'button'
responseTypes.use: Offer a small set of choices as buttons or a dropdown.
responseTypes.example: { response_type: MessageResponseTypes.OPTION, title: 'Choose', options: [{ label: 'A', value: { input: { text: 'A' } } }] }

responseTypes.use: A single action button (send text, fire an event, open a panel, or link out).
responseTypes.example: { response_type: MessageResponseTypes.BUTTON, label: 'Open docs', button_type: 'url', url: 'https://carbondesignsystem.com' }

responseTypes.fields: body?: GenericItem[], footer?: ButtonItem[], max_width?: 'small'|'medium'|'large' (CardItem = BaseGenericItem & WithBodyAndFooter & WithWidthOptions). NOTE: the field is 'body', NOT 'items'.
responseTypes.use: A bordered card grouping body content with an optional footer.
responseTypes.example: { response_type: MessageResponseTypes.CARD, body: [{ response_type: MessageResponseTypes.TEXT, text: 'Card body' }], footer: [{ response_type: MessageResponseTypes.BUTTON, label: 'Open', button_type: 'url', url: 'https://example.com' }] }

responseTypes.use: A horizontally swipeable set of items (typically cards).
Arrange items in a grid of rows/cells.
Render an image (video/audio are the same MediaItem shape with their own response_type).

responseTypes.fields: source, title?, alt_text?, file_accessibility? (same MediaItem shape as image)
source, title?, alt_text?, file_accessibility? (same MediaItem shape as image)

responseTypes.use: Embed an external page inline or in a panel.
Prompt the user for a date.
Render your OWN React/HTML via renderUserDefinedResponse (see the user-defined topic). The escape hatch for custom UI.
RAG answer: generated text with source citations.

responseTypes.fields: agent_available?: { message }, agent_unavailable?: { message }, message_to_human_agent?, transfer_info?. NOTE: the enum MEMBER is MessageResponseTypes.CONNECT_TO_HUMAN_AGENT (string value 'connect_to_agent').
responseTypes.use: Offer/start a human-agent handoff (pairs with serviceDeskFactory).
responseTypes.example: { response_type: MessageResponseTypes.CONNECT_TO_HUMAN_AGENT, agent_available: { message: 'Connecting you to an agent…' }, agent_unavailable: { message: 'No agents are available right now.' } }

responseTypes.use: Insert a typing pause between items for pacing.
Show an inline error within the transcript.
A status/informational system line in the transcript (standalone if the only item, else inline).
A card that opens an attached workspace panel.
responseTypes.example: { response_type: MessageResponseTypes.PREVIEW_CARD, workspace_id: 'ws-123', title: 'Order #4821', subtitle: 'Open details' }
